request.question:
Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.
In plain English: can we engineer baker's yeast to convert a sugar found in agricultural waste into fuel, something it cannot naturally do?

hypothesis.intervention: heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. (specifics of expression system TBD)
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: xylose isomerase Saccharomyces cerevisiae fermentation ethanol yield
hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.
In plain English: can we engineer baker's yeast to convert a sugar found in agricultural waste into fuel, something it cannot naturally do?

references.title: Metal Dependence of the Xylose Isomerase from Piromyces sp. E2 Explored by Activity Profiling and Protein Crystallography
references.relevance_note: This paper specifically discusses the xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. and its application in Saccharomyces cerevisiae for ethanol fermentation, which is directly relevant to the hypothesis.

literature_qc.explanation: The retrieved paper [4] discusses the xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. and its application in Saccharomyces cerevisiae for ethanol fermentation, which aligns directly with the intervention and biological system specified in the hypothesis. Additionally, the focus on ethanol production as an outcome matches the measurable outcome of the hypothesis, confirming that the core experiment is already described in the literature.
literature_qc.recommended_action: A paper matching this experiment was found in the scientific literature. Review it before proceeding. If this is intentional replication, state that explicitly. If not, differentiate your approach.

risks.description: Control strategy may miss process-specific artifacts in handling or reagent preparation.
risks.mitigation: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

plan.objective: To evaluate the ethanol yield from Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK expressing heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. compared to the parental strain.
plan.experimental_design: The experiment will involve fermenting xylose in a controlled bioreactor setup using both the engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain and the parental strain. Ethanol production will be measured over a defined fermentation period, and comparisons will be made to assess the impact of the heterologous xylose isomerase on ethanol yield.
controls: Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK parental strain fermented with 50 g/L xylose under anaerobic conditions.
Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK parental strain fermented with 50 g/L glucose under anaerobic conditions.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 1 L of YPD medium containing 20 g/L yeast extract, 20 g/L peptone, and 200 g/L deionized water. Adjust pH to 5.5 using 1 M HCl or NaOH. Sterilize by autoclaving at 121°C for 15 min.
Equipment: Autoclave (Thermo Scientific, Model 1000), pH meter (Hanna Instruments, Model HI 98107)
Reagents: 20 g/L yeast extract (BD Biosciences), 20 g/L peptone (BD Biosciences), 200 g/L deionized water
Expected outcome: Medium pH is adjusted to 5.5 and sterilized without contamination.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 24 h | Strain Preparation
Inoculate 10 mL of YPD medium with a single colony of Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK parental strain and incubate at 30°C for 24 h with shaking at 200 rpm.
Equipment: Incubator (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Model 1000), Shaker (New Brunswick Scientific, Model Innova 44)
Reagents: 10 mL YPD medium
Expected outcome: Culture reaches an OD600 of approximately 1.0 after 24 h incubation.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Transformation
Transform Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK with the plasmid containing the heterologous xylose isomerase gene from Piromyces sp. using the lithium acetate method. Prepare transformation mix with 100 µL of competent cells, 10 µg of plasmid DNA, and 240 µL of PEG 3350 solution. Incubate at 30°C for 30 min, then heat shock at 42°C for 15 min.
Equipment: Water bath (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Model 1000)
Reagents: 100 µL competent Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK, 10 µg plasmid DNA, 240 µL 50% PEG 3350 (Sigma-Aldrich)
Expected outcome: Successful transformation indicated by growth on selective media after plating.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Selection of Transformants
Plate 100 µL of transformed cells onto selective YPD agar plates containing 200 µg/mL G418. Incubate at 30°C for 48 h.
Equipment: Incubator (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Model 1000)
Reagents: Selective YPD agar plates with 200 µg/mL G418 (Sigma-Aldrich)
Expected outcome: Colonies appear on selective plates indicating successful transformation.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Colony Screening
Pick 5 colonies from the selective plates and inoculate each into 5 mL of YPD medium containing 200 µg/mL G418. Incubate at 30°C for 24 h with shaking at 200 rpm.
Equipment: Shaker (New Brunswick Scientific, Model Innova 44)
Reagents: 5 mL YPD medium with 200 µg/mL G418
Expected outcome: Cultures reach an OD600 of approximately 1.0 after 24 h incubation.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Plasmid Extraction
Extract plasmid DNA from 1 mL of overnight culture using the QIAprep Spin Miniprep Kit (Qiagen) following the manufacturer's protocol. Elute DNA in 50 µL of elution buffer.
Equipment: Microcentrifuge (Eppendorf, Model 5424)
Reagents: QIAprep Spin Miniprep Kit (Qiagen)
Expected outcome: Purified plasmid DNA with A260/A280 ratio between 1.8 and 2.0.

DAY 1 | 2 h | PCR Confirmation
Perform PCR using 50 ng of plasmid DNA as template, with primers specific for the xylose isomerase gene. Use 25 µL reaction volume with 1x Taq polymerase buffer, 0.2 mM dNTPs, 0.5 µM primers, and 1 U Taq polymerase (Thermo Fisher Scientific). Amplify with the following conditions: 95°C for 5 min, 35 cycles of 95°C for 30 s, 55°C for 30 s, 72°C for 1 min, and a final extension at 72°C for 5 min.
Equipment: Thermal cycler (Bio-Rad, Model T100)
Reagents: 1x Taq polymerase buffer (Thermo Fisher Scientific), 0.2 mM dNTPs (Thermo Fisher Scientific), 0.5 µM primers (IDT), 1 U Taq polymerase (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Expected outcome: PCR products of expected size confirmed by gel electrophoresis.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Gel Electrophoresis
Run 5 µL of PCR product on a 1% agarose gel in TAE buffer at 100 V for 30 min. Stain with 0.5 µg/mL ethidium bromide and visualize under UV light.
Equipment: Gel electrophoresis apparatus (Bio-Rad, Model Mini-Sub Cell GT), UV transilluminator (Bio-Rad, Model Gel Doc XR+)
Reagents: 1% agarose gel in TAE buffer, 0.5 µg/mL ethidium bromide (Sigma-Aldrich)
Expected outcome: Bands corresponding to the expected size of the xylose isomerase gene are visible on the gel.
Safety: Ethidium bromide is a mutagen; handle with gloves.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Fermentation Setup
Prepare 1 L of fermentation medium containing 50 g/L xylose, 5 g/L yeast extract, and 5 g/L peptone. Adjust pH to 5.5 and sterilize by autoclaving at 121°C for 15 min. Inoculate with 10% (v/v) of overnight culture of either the engineered strain or the parental strain.
Equipment: Autoclave (Thermo Scientific, Model 1000), pH meter (Hanna Instruments, Model HI 98107)
Reagents: 50 g/L xylose (Sigma-Aldrich), 5 g/L yeast extract (BD Biosciences), 5 g/L peptone (BD Biosciences)
Expected outcome: Fermentation medium is prepared and inoculated with the appropriate strain.

DAY 1 | 72 h | Fermentation
Ferment the inoculated medium in a 2 L bioreactor (Applikon, Model 2L) at 30°C, 200 rpm, and 1 vvm aeration for 72 h. Monitor pH and maintain at 5.5 using 1 M NaOH.
Equipment: Bioreactor (Applikon, Model 2L), pH controller (Applikon, Model BioController)
Reagents: 1 M NaOH (Sigma-Aldrich)
Expected outcome: Ethanol production is monitored over 72 h, with pH maintained at 5.5.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Ethanol Measurement
At 24, 48, and 72 h, take 1 mL samples from the bioreactor and centrifuge at 6,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C. Collect supernatant for ethanol analysis.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge
Expected outcome: Supernatant is collected for ethanol quantification.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Ethanol Quantification
Quantify ethanol concentration in the supernatant using gas chromatography (Agilent 7890B) with a DB-FFAP column. Inject 1 µL of sample and run with the following conditions: initial temperature 80°C, hold for 2 min, ramp to 200°C at 10°C/min, hold for 5 min.
Equipment: Gas chromatograph (Agilent, Model 7890B)
Expected outcome: Ethanol concentration is determined in g/L from the chromatogram.

DAY 2 | 2 h | Data Analysis
Calculate ethanol yield based on the concentration measured and the initial xylose concentration. Use the formula: Ethanol yield (g/g) = (g/L ethanol)/(g/L xylose). Perform statistical analysis using ANOVA to compare yields between strains.
Expected outcome: Ethanol yield is calculated and compared statistically between engineered and parental strains.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: The heterologous xylose isomerase is functionally expressed in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Ethanol production can be accurately measured using gas chromatography.
The fermentation conditions are optimal for both strains.
decision_criteria: Proceed if ethanol yield from engineered strain is ≥ 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose.
Proceed if statistical analysis shows significant difference (p < 0.05) between engineered and parental strains.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of xylose for all fermentations.
Maintain consistent inoculum size across all experiments.
Calibrate gas chromatograph before each use.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To evaluate the ethanol yield from Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK expressing heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. compared to the parental strain.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
